items.title: Workers to be axed, production slowed at one of WA's last coal mines

children.content: Premier Coal is set to cut jobs and production at its Collie operation, citing slowing demand for coal-fired power as a key factor.

gallery.altText: A long shot of a coal mine with a large truck in shot.
A long shot of a coal mine.

thumbnail.altText: A long shot of a coal mine with a large truck in shot.
A long shot of a coal mine.

items.title: 'Outrageous mess': About 2,000 AI road safety camera fines withdrawn in WA

image.altText: A screenshot of a fine issues for a seatbelt issue

children.content: About 2,000 fines valued at $1.1 million issued as a result of AI-assisted road safety cameras in WA have been withdrawn, six months after the devices were introduced.

items.title: Judge condemns 'outrageous' school stabbing planned on Snapchat

children.content: Two boys are sentenced for their roles in the stabbing of a 15-year-old boy at a Perth high school, offending the prosecutor said was part of a "game" formulated online.

image.altText: A yellow Snapchat app logo on a black smart phone.

items.title: Last chance for answers as police admit error in search for Nannup Four

children.content: The family of a mother and daughter who went missing, along with two others, in WA almost 20 years ago are pinning their hopes on a last-ditch effort to uncover the truth of what became of them.

image.altText: Two men, a woman and a child holding their hands in a prayer pose.

items.title: Homes damaged, roof ripped off as possible tornado strikes WA's South West

children.content: Winds of more than 100 kilometres an hour swept along WA's southern coast last night, with serious damage reported in Bunbury and Esperance.

gallery.altText: A brick home with damaged roof and front window
A wide shot of brick home with damaged roof

thumbnail.altText: A brick home with damaged roof and front window
A wide shot of brick home with damaged roof
A toppled fence during the overnight storm in Bunbu

items.title: Pressure on Department of Justice after second woman dies at Perth prison

children.content: Calls are mounting for a "transparent" government review of WA's Bandyup Women's Prison after a second Aboriginal inmate dies inside the facility in less than five weeks.

image.altText: A sign out the front of Bandyup Women's Prison

items.title: E-bike rider dies after colliding with public bus in Perth's north-east

children.content: A 50-year-old man dies at the scene after his e-bike is involved in a crash with a Transperth bus at an intersection in Swan View, with police appealing for witnesses.

image.altText: Blue and white tape across a road

items.title: 'Large crocodile' attacks man in WA's far north

children.content: A man in his 20s has been flown to a Perth hospital after he was attacked by a crocodile at a popular camping spot north of Broome.

image.altText: A remote traffic sign

items.title: Residents urged to stay in as burnt battery product may react with forecast rain

children.content: Emergency services say a fire at the Li-ion Energy site in Maddington in Perth's south-east  — the biggest blaze of its kind in WA's history — will be reviewed to inform future responses to lithium battery fires.

image.altText: Flames can be seen billowing from a building in an industrial area.

items.title: Why this man risked crocodiles, storms and jail to fish in Australian waters

children.content: Why do Indonesian crews keep fishing illegally in Australian waters when the activity is so perilous?

gallery.altText: A man in a green shirt looks to the right.

thumbnail.altText: A man in a green shirt looks to the right.

items.title: Boyd says other AFL players are struggling 'under the surface' after Hollands episode

children.content: Tom Boyd, former AFL footballer turned mental health advocate, says the high-pressure environment of professional football means many players are struggling with mental health issues "under the surface".

image.altText: Tom Boyd speaks during a tribute to Danny Frawley before an AFL game.

items.title: Researchers develop surfboard fins to repel sharks after recent attacks

children.content: A multi-million-dollar project run out of the University of Wollongong is working on fitting shark-deterrent technology into surfboard fins following recent shark attacks.

image.altText: Man sanding surfboard in lab, wearing facemask and protective glasses

items.title: Former footballer Barry Cable found not guilty of sexually abusing child

children.content: Former Australian Rules footballer Barry Cable has been found not guilty of sexually abusing a child in Perth in the 1960s.

items.title: US says it's 'on the brink' of deal with Iran, but uncertainty over peace talks remains

children.content: The White House says the United States is "on the brink" of a deal with Iran as the ceasefire deadline looms. Follow live.

image.altText: Leavitt speaks at the lectern of the White House press briefing room wearing a light green jacket

items.title: Father calls for accountability over 21-month-old son's death

children.content: The father of Sandipan Dhar, who died after not receiving a blood test at Joondalup Health Campus, says there is still a lack of accountability over his son's death.

image.altText: A boy dressed in winter clothes sitting on a black couch

children.content: The object formed when a meteorite slammed into Earth more than 800,000 years ago.

items.title: Tow truck driver weeps after sentencing for fatal crash

children.content: Ross David Gamage has been fined $5,000 and had his licence suspended for six months over a 2023 crash that killed a 45-year-old man near Waroona.

image.altText: Rpss Gamage walks out of the Bunbury Courthouse after the decision.

items.title: New Zealand prime minister survives confidence vote

children.content: New Zealand Prime Minister Christopher Luxon says he has the support of his party after calling and winning a vote of confidence in his leadership.

image.altText: A bald man in a blue suit with a light blue tie and a New Zealand fern leaf pin looks at the camera

items.title: More women like Celine are being diagnosed with ADHD. We're not sure why

children.content: Within the sharp increase of ADHD rates in Australia lies a hidden story — more women are being prescribed medication than men. Understanding why is more complicated.

articles.title: Think you have ADHD? Here's what getting assessed looks like

children.content: Who can diagnose ADHD, what a proper assessment should include, and the red flags to look out for.

image.altText: A close up of a person marking a page on a clipboard, sitting across from a person clasping their hands.

articles.title: The neighbourhoods where ADHD rates are skyrocketing

children.content: ADHD diagnoses in adults have exploded across Australia since 2017. For the first time, we have the data that shows where it is — and isn't — happening.

image.altText: A search bar saying "Enter your area" is superimposed on a map of Australia against a blue-tinged backdrop of medication.

items.title: Why the gas giants stopped gouging

children.content: After a decade and a half of mostly failed threats from three prime ministers and constant surveillance by the competition regulator, the east coast gas export giants are suddenly putting Australians first.

image.altText: A huge pill-shaped tank with giant red letters LNG on a cargo ship.

items.title: 'I know who my son is': Elijah Hollands's father posts heartfelt message

children.content: Elijah Hollands's father, former AFL player Ben Hollands, posts on social media pledging to help lift his son up and urging others to check on their friends if they are struggling.

image.altText: Elijah Hollands high fives AFL fans after a Carlton game.

items.title: Australia's biggest AI factory planned for remote WA

children.content: A huge artificial intelligence data centre capable of training models like ChatGPT is set to be built south of Broome in WA's Kimberley at a cost of billons of dollars.

items.title: Dezi Freeman's shipping container entered by YouTubers, police investigate

children.content: YouTubers are being investigated for trespass after filming inside the converted shipping container where fugitive gunman Dezi Freeman hid after shooting dead two police officers.

image.altText: Items inside a shipping container.

items.title: 'I had no-one to talk to': The 92yo trying to save his dying language

children.content: Peter is the last living speaker of the First Nations language Thiinma. When he met linguist Rosie, they used music to help keep his knowledge alive, and formed a beautiful friendship along the way.

image.altText: Peter stands on stage at a microphone with bright lights behind him, wearing an akubra, shirt and bolo tie

items.title: Six ways your smartwatch is lying to you, according to science

children.content: Wearable fitness technologies like smartwatches are worn by millions of people daily. But they're not as accurate as you might think.

image.altText: A woman in a red T-shirt looks down at a watch next to swimming pool

items.title: Living in an apartment with your dog? Here are four things to consider

children.content: Providing opportunities for your dog to explore, giving them a safe and comfortable space to retreat to — as well as a good routine — are key to keeping your dog happy.

image.altText: small brown dog laying in blue furry bed

items.title: Push to tax gas companies 'ripping up our oceans' as Gorgon plan approved

children.content: Natural gas taxes are back in the fray after the expansion of Chevron's Gorgon project off WA's north-west coast received environmental approval amid a Senate inquiry into how resources are taxed.

articles.title: Gas tax fight escalates ahead of budget as inquiry begins

children.content: Calls are growing for a new 25 per cent tax on gas exports to increase the petroleum resource rent tax on windfall profits, but companies have warned any changes would jeopardise Australia's energy supply.

image.altText: David Pocock at a press conference at Parliament House

items.title: F1 makes 'sensible changes' after engine concerns to start season

children.content: The next Formula 1 race in Miami will be a testing and proving ground for a number of new rules aimed at improving performance and safety.

items.title: King Charles pays tribute to 'darling Mama' on late queen's 100th birthday

children.content: The king's moving homage comes as the final master plan for the memorial for the nation's longest-serving monarch is unveiled.

items.title: Red-hot Perth ace Green blazes to third LA Championship triumph

children.content: Australian golf ace Hannah Green wins her fourth title of the season, taking out the LPGA Tour's LA Championship for a third time in four years.

image.altText: An Australian golfer smiles as she stands on a green holding a large trophy after winning an LPGA tournament.

items.title: This country music singer's tour costs have got her rethinking her career

children.content: Adele Oliver admits being a country music artist has "never been a great career choice", but she says things are worse than ever, with her last gig costing her money due to the escalating price of fuel.
